name: Alaska Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Alaska - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Dietitians and Nutritionists Program
provider.parentOrganization: Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing

address.streetAddress:
State Office Building, 333 Willoughby Ave., 9th Fl.
Juneau, AK 99801
address.description:
State of Alaska
Board of Dietitians and Nutritionists
PO Box 110806
Juneau AK 99811-0806

licenseDuration: Up to 2 Years (depending on issue date relative to the cycle)
renewalCycle: Every 2 years (expires Dec 31 of odd-numbered years)

requiredDocuments.name: Official Application & Photo
requiredDocuments.description: Application with notarized signature and a recent passport-style photograph.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Must be sent directly from the Commission on Dietetic Registration.

requiredDocuments.name: Verification of Out-of-State Licenses
requiredDocuments.description: If applying with existing licenses, verification from all other states must be submitted.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does an Alaska dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial cost is $150, which includes a $100 application fee and a $50 initial license fee.

mainEntity.name: When does the Alaska license need to be renewed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Alaska dietitian licenses renew biennially and expire on December 31st of odd-numbered years. The renewal period requires a $125 fee. There is no grace period for practicing on a lapsed license.

mainEntity.name: Does Alaska require a background check?

acceptedAnswer.text: While an extensive FBI fingerprint check is not centrally required for all applicants in this program, individuals must answer professional fitness questions and may need to provide court documents if they have a disciplinary history.

mainEntity.name: Does Alaska have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Alaska. To work in Alaska, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Alaska?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Alaska while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
